meta.title: Jalapão Gaviões do Cerrado — Adventure itineraries in Tocantins, Brazil
meta.description: Specialized travel boutique for Jalapão, Chapada das Mesas, and Serra Gerais. Human support on WhatsApp, well-paced wilderness trips rooted in Brazil’s cerrado biome.
meta.keywords: Jalapão, Tocantins, Chapada das Mesas, Serra Gerais, eco travel, Jalapão Gaviões do Cerrado, Brazil safari

hero.title: Experience Jalapão and Tocantins with guides shaped by road and wilderness.
hero.subtitle: Carefully crafted routes, safety first, sincere hospitality—from first WhatsApp ping to farewell.
hero.imageAlt: Aerial view of a fervedouro natural spring in Jalapão State Park, crystalline pool and cerrado surrounds

about.title: About us
about.lead: We chase wide silences, gold-hour light over savanna grasses, and the pulse of northern Brazil.
about.p1: Jalapão Gaviões do Cerrado curates itineraries that honour each landscape’s pacing, respecting communities and ecosystems while staying close via WhatsApp from planning to postcards home.
about.p2: We serve Jalapão, Chapada das Mesas, and Serra Gerais routes—regions that reinvent themselves with rainy and dry spells.

bullets.b1: Comfortable pacing for families and intimate groups
bullets.b2: Iconic must-sees plus local curveballs worth the detours
bullets.b3: Seasonal logistics, packing lists, and honest expectations

routesSection.title: Itineraries
routesSection.lead: Pick your highlight—we’ll already know why you knocked when WhatsApp pings.

jalapao.title: Jalapão
jalapao.description: State park staples—fervedouros, dunes, Cachoeira da Velha—with breathing room baked into every day.
jalapao.altCover: Jalapão river winding through cerrado greenery

panels.title: Day 1 — Palmas toward Jalapão
panels.body:
Morning departure from Palmas-Miracema toward Mateiros, with the landscape slowly opening into typical cerrado. Practical stops whenever they make sense—coffee, restrooms, quick horizon snaps.
Arrival in Mateiros late afternoon or early evening, depending on your flight and pickup time. Check-in at the lodge with time to settle in.
Easy chat about next days’ pacing—no frantic rush through dozens of spots in one trip; steady sun exposure and steady water intake, hats and sunscreen mandatory.
Dinner unrushed—light fare if tired—and timings for tomorrow squared away with your hosts.

panels.title: Day 2 — Rio Novo, fervedouros and first plunge
panels.body:
Outings toward crystal-clear stretches of the Rio Novo: fervedouros and sandy river beaches framed for longer swims—not drive-by glimpses.
Photography paced between dips so you savour a handful of stellar places.
Sit-down lunch at partner kitchens when routing allows.
Back near Mateiros before dark; squeeze in village sunset if everybody’s game without rushing supper.

panels.title: Day 3 — Fine dunes, lookouts & chapada sunsets
panels.body:
Route to towering golden dunes—soft climbs, ridges and lookouts layering Jalapão against thicker cerrado backdrops.
Walk at the collective pace—shade pockets, litres of liquids, guilt-free pauses when heat spikes.
If your customised plan includes plateau lookouts toward sunset we pick angles where light shifts redefine every snap.
Clear Mateiros nights—in great stargazing months it’s worth a skyward glance before bed.

panels.title: Day 4 — Cachoeira da Velha & monumental pools
panels.body:
Full sensory immersion at Cachoeira da Velha: generous standing time on sculpted rock, swims only where authorised partners and ranger guidance allow collective safety.
Depending on build, nearby pools slot in thoughtfully—never a frantic relay between endless stops.
Lunch midpoint per pre-trip logistics.
Return pleasantly spent after hours underwater and beneath open sun.

panels.title: Day 5 — Final touches or return to Palmas
panels.body:
On longer bundles this day keeps a lighter morning excursion (if slated) or is dedicated to structured farewell: slow village coffee and luggage audits before shuttle or chartered van to Palmas.
Same-day flight departures bake padding into the Palmas-Miracema run—wet seasons and surprise traffic deserve that buffer heading to the airport.

chapada-mesas.title: Chapada das Mesas
chapada-mesas.description: Amazon-inflected plateaus begging for wide-angle lenses—long days mirrored by luminous sunsets.
chapada-mesas.altCover: Chapada das Mesas open horizon with plateau vegetation

panels.title: Day 1 — Arrival & first contact with the chapada
panels.body:
Welcome in Santa Teresa do Tocantins or another strategic base near the Amazon-flavoured canyons, with lodging set up as hub for lookouts and signature trails.
Late afternoon we take a short walk near the inn to acclimate to heat and humidity along Tocantins’ Amazon-edge microclimate.
Initial sunset whenever routing allows—mesas already painting colours you’ll remember on camera.
Dinner with local kitchens featuring river fish and northern Brazilian sides; fine details of this leg we lock with you before arrival.

panels.title: Day 2 — Canyons under an open sky
panels.body:
Ascent to lookouts where the Amazon canyon opens at once: deep walls, wind aloft, that sense of scale defining the region.
Short geology and climate context—helpful to understand why rain may shuffle certain paths.
Photography unhurried; light pivots fast and each hour reshapes the relief—hats, water and sunscreen are non-negotiable under largely shadeless belvederes.
Lunch mid-day or after the core stretch per your closed itinerary; afternoon buffer at the inn before any optional add-on if energy and schedule align.

panels.title: Day 3 — Waterfalls, pools & swims below the falls
panels.body:
Focused on major drops and wide pools: moderate walks to swim spots when river level and trail grip keep everyone safe.
Heavy rain or slick stone may reshuffle the morning—group wellbeing and local guidance lead.
Long stretches riverside to swim, float or simply sit on sun-warmed rock listening to the cascade.
We may wrap earlier if water volume spikes in very wet windows—that’s part of travelling through a living landscape.

panels.title: Days 4–5 — Riverside communities, regional food & exit
panels.body:
When your package includes it: brief visits to river communities or small farms with mindful etiquette—fair pay for crafts and services, ask before photographing residents, quieter voices beside homes.
Home-style lunches underscore fish, manioc flours and Amazon-Tocantins seasonings.
Before the transfer back, roadside coffee plus luggage and documents reviewed calmly—built-in slack avoids scrambling before flights or lengthy connections.
Final timings toward Palmas or other hubs only after pre-trip planning because holidays, crowds and rains stretch drive times noticeably.

serra-gerais.title: Serra Gerais
serra-gerais.description: Vibrant communities, braided rivers, trekking depth—classic cerrado energy with hospitable pacing.
serra-gerais.altCover: Rolling cerrado ridges in Serra Gerais sunlight

panels.title: Day 1 — Crossing into the cerrado territory
panels.body:
Transfer toward your base near Natividade or whichever municipality anchors lodging and the community, trail and state-sector segments spelled out in your contract.
Scenic stops where the chapadão first breaks the skyline—classic campo sujo, ipês, rocky outcrops and cerrado to the horizon.
We baseline visitor habits: carry trash out, keep voices low by homes, photograph residents only after clear consent.
Welcome dinner showcasing Tocantins flavours where menus allow plus a straight talk about next morning departure while daylight still safeguards trailhead access—no gratuitous dawn alarms.

panels.title: Day 2 — Quilombo communities & cerrado trails
panels.body:
When included we visit communal or quilombo nuclei ethically—listen openly, honour crafts with fair pricing, steer clear of aggressive haggling.
Moderate walks where buritis and creeks braid through cerrado wildlife moments favour telephoto silence over noisy pursuit.
Contracted creek swims happen solely where guides and local norms confirm safe depth and current.
We return early enough for showers, supper and hammock time swapping stories about what crossed the trail.

panels.title: Day 3 — Last outings or mellow farewell
panels.body:
Depending on packaging: lookout at dawn, quick splash at a neighbouring pool—or slow coffee by the courtyard with luggage squared away.
Farm visits or artisan studios stay appointment-based because hosts juggle hospitality with other livelihoods.
Hearty farewell lunch; even easy-going cerrado itineraries rack up footsteps—dishes such as hearty rice mixes appear when seasonal produce allows.
Return transfer with suitcase and passport double-check plus scheduled slack so flights home don’t reopen the stress valve.

panels.title: Days 4–7 — Rio São Miguel, longer hikes or festive dates
panels.body:
Extended stays weave Rio São Miguel outings across clearly defined public segments with partner boat crews, respecting capacity caps, life jackets and safety briefings tightened during rainy surges.
Multi-day trekking gently ramps kilometres with shade breaks—we recommend two litres minimum per adult and refilling at base every sunrise before heading out.
Layering regional festivals onto nature-focused trips only happens with explicit cultural interest because crowds, lodging and highways diverge massively from quieter cerrado circuits.
Every extra night finalises alongside you beforehand—rainy season rhythms, conditioning and optimum months for specific segments steer the customised plan.

testimonials.title: Guest voices
testimonials.lead: What travelers replay most: fewer rushed checklists, more connection to place.

items.name: Marina Costa
items.quote: Perfect rhythm—time to float in fervedouros, chase dawn light, and unwind at night. Quick answers on WhatsApp every step.

items.name: Ricardo & Paula
items.quote: We traveled with kids and felt supported from planning to our last Chapada stop. Logistics never felt stressful.

items.name: Anete Souza
items.quote: An honest pick for anyone tired of cookie-cutter packages. We’re already plotting the Serra Gerais return.

contact.title: Contact
contact.lead: Share travel dates, party size, and dream destination—we reply fast on WhatsApp.
contact.location: Tocantins, Brazil · Jalapão, Chapada das Mesas & Serra Gerais
contact.midPageSubtitle: One WhatsApp note is enough to kick things off.

ctaBanner.title: Ready to map your next adventure?
ctaBanner.subtitle: Open a chat and we’ll sketch a proposal around your calendar.
